item.title: US Stock Futures Fall, Oil Climbs on Iran Tensions: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stock futures fell in early Friday trading and oil rose as escalating conflict in the Middle East rattled investors and revived concerns about energy supplies.

item.title: Oil Jumps Following Fresh Clashes Between US and Iranian Forces
item.description: Oil jumped following renewed clashes between US and Iranian forces, jeopardizing the outlook for a deal to end the 10-week war.

item.title: SoftBank Rally Hinges on OpenAI Growth Easing Balance Sheet Fear
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s stock rally faces a test against reality next week, with investors clamoring for assurance that the company’s multibillion-dollar bet on an increasingly embattled OpenAI will be worth the money.

item.title: $10 Billion Golub Fund Caps Outflows After Requests for 8.5%
item.description: Golub Capital is capping withdrawals from its private credit fund after investors sought to pull 8.5% of shares, the latest instance of a money manager restricting outflows amid a wave of redemption requests.

item.title: Blackstone-Led Group Set to Inject $100 Million Into Medallia
item.description: A group of private credit investors led by Blackstone Inc. is preparing to inject at least $100 million of fresh funds into Medallia Inc. as part of a restructuring that will put them in control of the software maker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wynn Expects ‘Modest Delay’ in Opening of New Middle East Casino
item.description: Wynn Resorts Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Craig Billings said the company now expects a “modest delay” in the opening date of a new casino resort it’s building in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Cloudflare Forecast Misses Estimates, Announces Job Cuts
item.description: Cloudflare Inc. stock fell on Thursday after the company said it would slash jobs and gave a forecast for revenue that fell short of analysts’ expectations.

item.title: The AI Revival of the Three Mile Island Nuclear Plant
item.description: Microsoft’s vast power demands are bringing together two transformative—and risky—technologies.

item.title: World’s Most Important Bromance Revived as Trump Heads to China
item.description: When Donald Trump’s plane touches down in Beijing, he’ll become the first sitting US president to visit China in nearly a decade. His two-day summit with Xi Jinping extends a personal relationship that’s weathered a tariff war, pandemic and historic energy crisis.

item.title: Cloudflare to Cut One-Fifth of Workers in Move to AI-First Model
item.description: Cloudflare Inc. plans to cut more than 1,100 jobs globally as it accelerates its shift to an agentic AI-first operating model.

item.title: DraftKings Profit Beats Estimates as Sportsbook Sales Grow
item.description: DraftKings Inc. reported revenue that rose 17% to $1.65 billion, slightly ahead of analysts’ estimates for $1.63 billion.

item.title: Nvidia to Invest Up to $2.1 Billion in Data Center Firm IREN
item.description: Nvidia Corp. is investing as much as $2.1 billion in the data center developer IREN Ltd. as part of a broader partnership between the two companies aimed at accelerating the construction of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: The Fed’s Susan Collins on FOMC Dissent and the Warsh Transition
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast, Boston Fed President Susan Collins on recent FOMC dissent, Kevin Warsh’s nomination and where interest rates could land in 2026.

item.title: Elliott Said to Tap Banks to Run Barnes & Noble’s London IPO
item.description: Elliott Management Corp. has selected banks to lead a combined initial public offering of book retailers Barnes & Noble Inc. and Waterstones Booksellers Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Coinbase Swings to a Loss While Posting Another Revenue Drop
item.description: Just days after announcing deep job cuts made to manage costs in volatile markets and to harness advances in artificial intelligence, Coinbase Global Inc. showed how substantially a lingering crypto bear market can weigh on results.

item.title: CoreWeave Posts Sales Surge, Wider Loss After Spending Boom
item.description: AI data center operator CoreWeave Inc. posted higher sales than anticipated last quarter, but also a wider loss, after the company spent heavily to bolster its operations and meet burgeoning demand.

item.title: Dorsey’s Block Raises Forecasts After AI-Driven Job Cuts
item.description: Block Inc. offered a sunnier outlook for profits and growth on Thursday after orchestrating a severe round of job cuts related to artificial intelligence that executives said were painful but necessary to improve performance.

item.title: Airbnb Boosts 2026 Revenue Outlook on Increased Bookings
item.description: Airbnb Inc. boosted its revenue growth forecast for the year on “particularly strong” bookings in the Americas and benefits from simplifying its fee structure for hosts and guests.

item.title: Expedia Revenue Beat Shows Middle East Resilience, Eyes World Cup
item.description: Expedia Group Inc. posted quarterly revenue that came in slightly ahead of estimates as it overcame Middle East-related travel disruptions and flagged healthy demand from this summer’s FIFA World Cup.

item.title: MercadoLibre Slips As Profits Miss Despite Revenue Surge
item.description: Shares in e-commerce and fintech giant MercadoLibre Inc. slipped after profits missed estimates for the fourth quarter in a row even as revenue grew at its fastest pace in nearly four years.

item.title: IEA Head Calls on Canada to Move Faster on Energy Amid Supply Shock
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency is urging Canada to move more quickly to develop and export its energy resources, warning that market conditions will only favor the country for so long.

item.title: Ex-Willkie Lawyer Pleaded Guilty in M&A Insider-Trading Case
item.description: A former Willkie Farr & Gallagher counsel is cooperating with prosecutors after pleading guilty to being one of the elite mergers and acquisitions lawyers who provided tips on deals to a massive insider trading ring.

item.title: Latin America’s Largest Economy Sets Export Record on Oil Price Surge
item.description: Brazil set a monthly export record in April as war in the Middle East propelled oil prices higher, according to government data published on Thursday.

item.title: Private Credit’s Birthplace Gives New Rise to 1980s-Style Greed
item.description: Attendees walk past signage of the Milken Institute during the 29th annual Milken Institute Global Conference at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, California on May 5, 2026.

item.title: GameStop's eBay Bid Is So Befuddling Even Merger Arbs Are Steering Clear
item.description: On paper, GameStop Corp.’s head-turning $56 billion bid for eBay Inc. offers a potential fat profit for investors willing to wager on the unconventional deal. In practice, merger specialists aren’t buying it.

item.title: US Consumer Borrowing Increased in March by Most Since 2022
item.description: US consumer borrowing increased in March by the most since late 2022, reflecting a surge in non-revolving credit as well as higher credit-card balances.

item.title: Chilean President Kast’s Mega Economic Bill Clears First Hurdle as Trouble Brews
item.description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast’s flagship economic bill cleared its first congressional hurdle on Thursday, but the vote gives the new administration little respite as it works to muster legislative support.

item.title: La informalidad impulsa el reinado del efectivo en México
item.description: La arraigada cultura del pago en efectivo del país se mantiene firme frente a un esfuerzo implacable de cientos de millones de dólares por parte de las empresas de tecnología financiera

item.title: Banxico Cuts Rate to 6.5% in Possible End to Easing Cycle
item.description: Mexico reduced borrowing costs by a quarter of a percentage point, a move that the central bank’s chief signaled could mark the end of the easing cycle that began two years ago.

item.title: Trump’s Summit With Xi Is On Despite China’s Iran Concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump is forging ahead with plans to hold a summit with counterpart Xi Jinping next week in Beijing, despite unease among Chinese officials about holding the high-stakes meeting before the war in Iran is resolved.
